title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. squarrosa
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. squarrosa, also known as the Poison Tree, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of the Pacific and Indian Oceans. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, attributed to its high content of coumarins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Excoecaria biglandulosa var. squarrosa include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, as well as its potential to protect against oxidative stress and cell damage. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a decoction, but caution should be exercised due to the plant's toxic properties and potential side effects.
